questions.q: In which year was the Cripps Mission sent to India?
questions.explanation: The Cripps Mission arrived in India in March 1942 during the Second World War.

questions.q: After whom was the Cripps Mission named?
questions.explanation: The mission was led by Sir Stafford Cripps, a senior British Labour politician.

questions.q: What status did the Cripps Mission offer India after the war?
questions.explanation: The mission offered India dominion status after the war in return for wartime cooperation.

questions.q: What was the Cripps Mission asking Indians to provide in exchange for its offer?
questions.explanation: The proposals sought full Indian cooperation in the war effort in return for post-war constitutional change.

questions.q: Which famous phrase was used to dismiss the Cripps offer?
questions.explanation: The offer was famously dismissed as a 'post-dated cheque,' meaning a promise deferred to after the war.

questions.q: Which major Indian party rejected the Cripps proposals?
questions.explanation: The Indian National Congress rejected the Cripps proposals.

questions.q: The Cripps Mission was dispatched during which global conflict?
questions.explanation: The mission came in 1942, in the midst of the Second World War.

questions.q: Which country's political party did Stafford Cripps belong to?
questions.explanation: Cripps was a leading member of the British Labour Party serving in the wartime coalition.

questions.q: A 'post-dated cheque' implies the British promise of self-government was to be honoured when?
questions.explanation: The metaphor mocked the deferral of the offer until after the war was over.

questions.q: Did the Cripps Mission succeed in winning Indian support for the war effort?
questions.explanation: The mission ended in failure, with the main parties rejecting its proposals.

questions.q: Which British Prime Minister sent Stafford Cripps to India in 1942?
questions.explanation: Winston Churchill's wartime government dispatched Cripps to India in 1942.

questions.q: What external pressure pushed Britain to send the Cripps Mission in early 1942?
options: Japanese military advances in Southeast Asia
questions.explanation: Japanese advances after the fall of Singapore and Burma pressured Britain to secure Indian support.

questions.q: What controversial provision allowed provinces to opt out of the proposed Indian Union?
options: The right of provinces not to accede
questions.explanation: The proposals allowed any province to stay out of the Union, which nationalists feared could lead to partition.

questions.q: Why did Congress object strongly to the defence arrangements in the Cripps offer?
options: Defence was given entirely to the Muslim League
Defence was to remain under British control
There was no mention of defence
questions.explanation: Congress demanded a meaningful share in defence, but the British insisted on retaining control of it during the war.

questions.q: Who is most often credited with calling the Cripps offer a 'post-dated cheque'?
questions.explanation: Gandhi is widely credited with the 'post-dated cheque' description, though variations are attributed to others.

questions.q: What body did the Cripps proposals promise would be set up after the war to frame a constitution?
options: A constitution-making body or constituent assembly
questions.explanation: The proposals promised an elected constitution-making body to draft India's post-war constitution.

questions.q: Why did the All-India Muslim League also reject the Cripps proposals?
options: They did not concede Pakistan explicitly
questions.explanation: The League was dissatisfied because the proposals did not clearly concede the demand for Pakistan.

questions.q: The failure of the Cripps Mission was a direct trigger for which mass movement?
questions.explanation: The breakdown of the Cripps Mission led directly to the launch of the Quit India Movement in August 1942.

questions.q: How did the British view India's role within the Empire in the Cripps offer of dominion status?
options: As a directly ruled colony forever
As a self-governing dominion like Canada or Australia
As an independent republic at once
questions.explanation: Dominion status would place India on a footing similar to self-governing dominions such as Canada and Australia.

questions.q: Which famous American figure took an interest in the Cripps negotiations, reflecting US pressure on Britain?
questions.explanation: President Roosevelt pressed Churchill to address Indian demands, sending a personal envoy during the talks.

questions.q: What was the outcome for British-Indian relations after the Cripps Mission failed?
questions.explanation: The failure deepened mistrust and led to the confrontation of the Quit India Movement.

questions.q: In which month of 1942 did Stafford Cripps arrive in India to present his proposals?
questions.explanation: Cripps reached Delhi in March 1942 and presented the draft declaration that month.

questions.q: Which Congress leader negotiated extensively with Cripps over the defence portfolio in 1942?
questions.explanation: As Congress president, Maulana Azad led the negotiations with Cripps, including on the defence question.

questions.q: Which American envoy sent by Roosevelt was present in India during the Cripps talks?
questions.explanation: Roosevelt's personal representative Louis Johnson was in India and involved himself in the Cripps negotiations.

questions.q: Churchill's Secretary of State for India during the Cripps Mission was whom?
questions.explanation: Leo Amery served as Secretary of State for India in Churchill's wartime government in 1942.

questions.q: The Cripps proposals envisaged that Indian states would relate to the new Union through what arrangement?
options: Signing a treaty of accession with the Union
questions.explanation: Princely states were to be able to negotiate their relationship and accede to the Union by treaty rather than by compulsion.

questions.q: What did Congress propose instead of the British retaining the defence portfolio, leading to a deadlock?
options: Handing defence to the United States
Abolishing the army during the war
An Indian member responsible for defence in the government
questions.explanation: Congress wanted a meaningful Indian role over defence, but the deadlock over its scope helped break the talks.

questions.q: Which Viceroy was in office and worked alongside Cripps during the 1942 mission?
questions.explanation: Lord Linlithgow was Viceroy in 1942 during the Cripps Mission and resisted concessions on defence.

questions.q: The proposed provision allowing non-acceding provinces to form their own union was seen by Congress as the seed of what?
questions.explanation: Congress viewed the opt-out clause as containing the principle of partition, which it strongly opposed.

questions.q: After the failure, in what year did a later British delegation, the Cabinet Mission, again attempt to resolve the constitutional question?
questions.explanation: The Cabinet Mission of 1946 was a subsequent British effort following the failed Cripps Mission of 1942.
